---
title: ""
page-layout: full
---

<div id="site-header">
<h1>Dog Capital</h1>
<p>Systematic strategies for the Argentine investor</p>
</div>

<div id="site-nav">
<a href="#about">About</a>
<a href="#programs">Programs</a>
<a href="#research">Research</a>
<a href="#contact">Contact</a>
</div>

<div class="section" id="about">

## About

Dog Capital builds and runs systematic investment programs for Argentine investors.

The core idea is simple: the S&P 500 is the best long-term asset available, and
volatility is the best tool to manage it. Rather than trying to time markets, we
use realized volatility to size positions dynamically — scaling in when markets
are calm, scaling out when they are not.

All programs operate via instruments available to Argentine retail investors:
CEDEARs on Balanz, and futures via international brokers. There is no black box —
signal logic, backtest evidence, and live performance are published openly.

</div>

<div class="section" id="programs">

## Programs

<div class="program-card">

### SPY Core

<span class="tag live">Live</span>
<span class="tag">CEDEARs</span>
<span class="tag">Monthly</span>
<span class="tag">No leverage</span>

Monthly accumulation into SPY CEDEARs via Balanz, sized by downside volatility
targeting. When realized volatility is low the strategy deploys capital fully;
when it rises, it scales back. Idle capital earns the dólar-linked rate.

Designed for Argentine investors building USD-denominated savings over the long term.

**Backtest (2001–2026):** CAGR 9.45% · Sharpe 0.693 · Max DD -36.97%

[Strategy document →](programs/spy-core.qmd)

</div>

</div>

<div class="section" id="research">

## Research

Strategy logic, parameter calibration, and backtest methodology are documented
in the program pages above. The framework — downside semi-deviation targeting,
signal smoothing, no-trade band, additions-only execution — is the same across
all programs.

</div>

<div class="section" id="contact">

## Contact

Independent. Not a fund. Open to conversations.

[papesjuan@gmail.com](mailto:papesjuan@gmail.com)

</div>

<div id="site-footer">
&copy; 2026 Dog Capital &nbsp;&middot;&nbsp; Buenos Aires &nbsp;&middot;&nbsp; Last updated: June 2026
</div>
